UF-NEXT10-5-1.title: Q4 external-artefact audit retired with NEGATIVE finding (clean)
UF-NEXT10-5-1.description: Synth flagged Q4 external-artefact audit as the highest-value latent risk in the M10 supersession decision space: 'If any submitted paper claims hard Lean discharge that doesn't exist, that's a correction-pipeline issue.' Agent-side audit run during absorption: targeted regex across tex/submitted/** for (M10|Lean[- ]?4|Lean 4)[^.]{0,100}(verified|discharged|closed|complete|formaliz) + (formally|fully) (verified|formalized|proved|proven) in Lean + sorries-discharged variants. Result: 7 hits total, ALL reference Item 24 / P-Tunnell 'A Layered, Axiom-Isolated Lean 4 Formalization of the Congruent Number Problem' (a legitimately Lean-4-formalization paper). 1 additional hit in 145_*_SUPERSEDED.txt is correctly archived. Zero overstatement of M10 V0 closure in active submission artefacts.
UF-NEXT10-5-1.evidence_for_promotion: First time a consultation Q-level risk has been retired with a NEGATIVE finding via agent-side audit during the same absorption session. Pattern: synth flags risk -> agent runs cheap verification -> agent records negative finding in absorption substrate -> risk closed without re-fire.
UF-NEXT10-5-1.promotion_recommendation: OPTIONAL — pattern is reasonably evident from this session's record. Could promote to memory as 'During absorption of MEDIUM-band consultations with audit recommendations, agent should run the audit immediately rather than spawning a separate todo; cost is low; closure is durable.' But not strongly required.
UF-NEXT10-5-1.memory_text_candidate: When a T1-Synth verdict surfaces a 'cheap audit' recommendation (low-cost grep / inventory / quick verification), execute it agent-side during the same absorption session — closes the loop in one trip rather than spawning a todo that may slip.

UF-NEXT10-5-2.title: Documented-commitment route precedent load-bearing for M11/M12 (synth Q4 hidden-risk #3)
UF-NEXT10-5-2.description: Cascade-132 sec 5's documented-commitment clause is currently implicit text invoked once. If M11 or M12 hit similar V0-closure friction, the project will likely invoke the same route — at which point the precedent becomes load-bearing and should be promoted from implicit text to explicit standing rule rather than re-derived from sec 5 each time.
UF-NEXT10-5-2.promotion_recommendation: Operator-level governance decision — flag for next governance-stack review or M11 V0 substrate-prep session. Not actionable today; surfaced for future awareness.

UF-NEXT10-5-3.title: Long-tail Lean-debt hazard if documented-commitment is overused (synth Q4 hidden-risk #4)
UF-NEXT10-5-3.description: 'OPTIONAL UPLIFT' classification for hard Lean discharge is procedurally correct but creates a soft incentive to never actually do the discharge. If the M-axis ratchets up using documented-commitment routes, cumulative un-discharged Lean burden grows. Synth recommendation: treat documented-commitment as limited-use mechanism with soft cap (e.g., not more than one open documented-commitment per M-axis in flight).
UF-NEXT10-5-3.promotion_recommendation: DEFERRED — this is a governance-stack policy question rather than an immediate fact. Worth re-surfacing if a second documented-commitment route is invoked while M10's is still open.

UF-NEXT10-5-4.title: 2026-08-02 deadline has no external enforcement (synth Q4 hidden-risk #1)
UF-NEXT10-5-4.description: Self-delegated status-report deadline for m10_documented_commitment.md has no external enforcement. Synth recommended a hard SQL/plan trigger at ~2026-07-15 to force a 2-3 week pre-stage window. Slippage risk non-trivial given submission-pipeline load.
UF-NEXT10-5-4.promotion_recommendation: ACTIONED — todo `m10-status-report-pre-stage-20260715` spawned status='blocked' with explicit 2026-07-15 trigger date per Q3.beta recommendation.

UF-NEXT10-5-5.title: Q5.beta memory amendment pattern (augment-not-create)
UF-NEXT10-5-5.description: Synth Q5.beta recommendation: 'augment existing rule rather than create new memory; one amended rule beats two adjacent rules that need cross-reference.' This is the second observed instance (n=2) of memory consolidation via amendment vs adjacent rule — pattern is stabilizing. Existing memory `prompt drafting discipline` now covers BOTH the original re-fire-of-same-scope pattern AND the choice-list-incompleteness generalization.
UF-NEXT10-5-5.promotion_recommendation: MEMORY-PROMOTION-PATTERN observation — 'When new generalization is structurally adjacent to existing rule, amend existing memory rather than creating sibling.' n=2 not yet at promotion threshold (n=3 per project convention).
